nodes.label: Hub-and-Spoke Model
nodes.description: The recommended information architecture pattern where the central student hub (students.unimelb.edu.au) holds the transactional spine and canonical content, while faculties maintain genuinely discipline-specific content with consistent cross-linking.

nodes.label: Transactional Admin Spine
nodes.description: The centrally-owned set of services including enrolment, exams/results, special consideration, fees/finance, and library — governed by university policy and delivered via students.unimelb.edu.au. The single clearest consolidation target in the estate per the crawl audit.

nodes.label: Topic Classification System
nodes.description: A controlled 22-tag vocabulary used to classify all crawled pages: enrolment, course-planning, subjects-timetable, exams-results, special-consideration, fees-finance, scholarships, placements-WIL, careers-employability, academic-skills, student-life, clubs-events, wellbeing-health, IT-systems, library, graduation, forms-admin, contacts-support, research-candidature, international, inclusion-equity, orientation.

nodes.label: BFS Crawl Methodology
nodes.description: An in-page fetch()-based breadth-first search using DOMParser, operating on same-origin faculty subdomains. Pages classified into unique, mixed, link-farm, or redirect/broken. Capped at depth 6 and 250 pages per faculty. Chrome baseline of ~21 hub links per page filtered out to avoid false 'mixed' classification.

nodes.label: students.unimelb.edu.au (Central Hub)
nodes.description: The central student portal and canonical source of truth for transactional admin, wellbeing, and course administration. Authority over enrolment, census dates, overloading, class timetable, cross-institutional study, special consideration, fees, and library services.

nodes.label: MyTimetable
nodes.description: The central class scheduling system used to create and view class timetables. Hub-owned but re-hosted/restated by faculties (FEIT, Arts).

nodes.label: SONIA Placement Platform
nodes.description: Placement management system used by MSD for internships and vocational placements, with ABP-specific processes.

nodes.label: Policy MPF1326 (Special Consideration)
nodes.description: University policy governing special consideration applications — a single central process restated across ~25 faculty pages.

nodes.label: Policy MPF1345 (Fitness to Practice)
nodes.description: University policy governing student fitness to practice for clinical placement courses, operationalised at faculty level (MDHS).

nodes.label: handbook.unimelb.edu.au (University Handbook)
nodes.description: The canonical subject and course database. Law relies on it heavily (801 links). Faculties delegate canonical course/subject data to the Handbook.

nodes.label: MSD Studio Archive
nodes.description: A 169+ page catalogue of individual design studio briefs from 2008-2026, deeply self-interlinked (~900+ URLs total). The single largest source of archival bloat in the entire crawl — 204 subjects-timetable pages and 60 student-life pages originate from MSD, mostly from this archive.

nodes.label: Science SSRC Committee Member Bios
nodes.description: ~30 individual committee-member biography pages for the Science Student-Staff Reference Committee (Hannah Ramsey, Kylie Wang, Viraj Patel, etc.), inflating the student-life topic count by roughly a third without adding student-facing guidance.

nodes.label: Faculty Restatement Pattern
nodes.description: Faculties restate hub-owned transactional content (special consideration, census dates, overloading, enrolment) rather than linking to the canonical hub pages. Most pronounced in special consideration (25 pages across faculties for one central process) and enrolment (70% restatement/redirect).

nodes.label: MDHS Compliance Estate
nodes.description: 44 MDHS pages tagged as wellbeing-health are actually clinical placement compliance: immunisations (Hepatitis B, MMR, Tuberculosis, Varicella, Pertussis, Influenza, COVID-19), screening checks (WWCC, Police Check, NDIS), and fitness-to-practice machinery. Misclassified under wellbeing but genuinely faculty-owned.

nodes.label: MDHS Fragmentation
nodes.description: MDHS has the most fragmented CS architecture: 3+ school subdomains (Biomedical Sciences, Dental, MSPGH) each run separate CS sections with different URL conventions (/study/current-students, /study/current-student-information, /study/student-resources). A Biomedicine student navigates 3+ different CS experiences.

nodes.label: URL Convention Inconsistency
nodes.description: Three different URL patterns exist across faculties: /students (5/9: Arts, FEIT, FBE, Law, Science), /current-students (MSD, FFAM), /study/current-students (Education, MDHS). MBS runs on a separate subdomain entirely. No standardisation.

nodes.label: Pastoral Wellbeing Duplication
nodes.description: Six faculties (FEIT, Science, FBE, MBS, Law, Education) each maintain near-identical 'Wellbeing' landing pages restating the same central counselling, crisis, and Safer Community services. The clearest consolidation candidate in wellbeing.

nodes.label: Law GRD Profile Noise
nodes.description: ~15 Law graduate-researcher profile pages (Andrea Furger, Marcus Hickleton, Charlie Hock, etc.) are false positives in exams-results and transactional-admin topics, swept in by stray keyword matches on 'academic-support-and-wellbeing' paths.

nodes.label: Arts Hub-Linking Pattern (Best Practice)
nodes.description: Arts is the model for hub-and-spoke: its census-dates, overloading, cross-institutional-study, and class-timetable pages are literally the hub's own course-admin/* URLs picked up because Arts deep-links to them rather than restating. The proof that hub consolidation works.

nodes.label: Domain Signal Finding
nodes.description: Of 227 transactional-admin pages, 219 live on faculty/school domains and only 6 on students.unimelb.edu.au — yet the hub is the source of truth. The faculty estate is the echo, not the source.

nodes.label: FEIT Self-Contained Ecosystem
nodes.description: FEIT runs the most complete parallel ecosystem: 204 pages with 0 link-farm classification, its own internship infrastructure (~14K words across 3 pages), orientation program, scholarship system, career mentoring, and graduate research support. Only 265 hub links across 204 pages.

nodes.label: FFAM Autonomy Case
nodes.description: FFAM operates almost entirely outside the central hub framework — thinnest root page (~100 words), no 'Where to go' guidance, conservatorium/art-school model does not map to hub service taxonomy. The strongest case for preserving faculty autonomy within the hub-and-spoke model.

nodes.label: FBE-MBS Split Architecture
nodes.description: FBE is unique in splitting students by cohort: BCom → FBE subdomain, graduate → MBS completely separate subdomain with its own branding, CS section, and career service (MBS Career Elevation). No cross-linking observed between the two.

nodes.label: Biomedical Major Redirect Issue
nodes.description: 16 of 42 Biomedical Sciences CS pages are redirects: major pages that immediately bounce current students to the prospective-student catalogue (study.unimelb.edu.au). 14 of 16 carry '2' suffix slugs indicating duplicate/versioned CMS slugs. A 'University Health Hub' link redirects to generic MDHS Contact page — effectively broken.

nodes.label: ABP Cloudflare Bypass Technique
nodes.description: In-page fetch() BFS from a tab loaded on msd.unimelb.edu.au defeated Cloudflare/403 blocks that hit WebFetch and direct navigation. All 244 content fetches returned HTTP 200. 250-page cap hit with 737 unvisited MSD Studio Archive detail URLs.

nodes.label: Placement Compliance Topic
nodes.description: Proposed re-tagging: MDHS's immunisation, screening checks, and fitness-to-practice content should be separated from 'wellbeing-health' into a dedicated placement/clinical-compliance topic. Currently 44 of 87 wellbeing pages are compliance, not pastoral.

nodes.label: Faculty Communities Gateway Page
nodes.description: Proposed hub page at students.unimelb.edu.au/student-life/faculty-communities — a thin index linking each faculty's student-experience hub so students can find 'what's on in my faculty' from the centre. Currently faculty communities are invisible from the hub.

nodes.label: Subjects & Timetabling Gateway
nodes.description: Proposed hub page that links every faculty's subject catalogue and MyTimetable. The live timetabling spine should be separated from archival subject bloat (MSD's 130+ archive pages should not sit in the same content class as census dates).

nodes.label: Health & Wellbeing Gateway Page
nodes.description: Proposed canonical entry at students.unimelb.edu.au/health-and-wellbeing. Each faculty 'Wellbeing' landing page should reduce to a thin signpost (local events + link to hub) rather than restating central services.

nodes.label: One Admin Landing Per Faculty
nodes.description: Each faculty should expose one consistent 'Enrolment, results & admin' landing page whose job is to link to the hub's transactional pages and surface genuine local exceptions (forms, hurdles, extensions). One label, one location, across all eight faculties.

nodes.label: URL Standardisation Recommendation
nodes.description: Redirect /current-students and /study/current-students to the /students convention used by 5/9 faculties. MDHS school subdomains should adopt a single pattern.

nodes.label: MSD De-Archive Recommendation
nodes.description: Move the MSD studio archive (2008-2018 Past Studios/Studio Archive pages) out of the current-students estate into a separate showcase/archive. Re-classify studios as subject/portfolio content. Collapse the Science SSRC committee roster behind its parent page. Together removes ~60 low-value pages from student-life and shrinks subjects-timetable by ~1/3.

nodes.label: MDHS Consolidation Recommendation
nodes.description: Consolidate school CS sections: Biomedical Sciences, Dental, and MSPGH CS should either redirect to a unified MDHS CS page or adopt the same template and URL convention. Fix circular links. Highest priority for consolidation — MDHS fragmentation is the worst.

nodes.label: Classifier Clean-Up Recommendation
nodes.description: Clean the classifier signal: exclude ~15 Law GRD profile false positives, MSD studio-archive from transactional topics, and stop tagging MSD studio archive and Science committee-member bios as 'student life'. Retag MDHS compliance as placement topic, not wellbeing.

nodes.label: Architecture, Building & Planning / MSD
nodes.description: Faculty of Architecture, Building and Planning operating as Melbourne School of Design. CS at msd.unimelb.edu.au/current-students. 250 pages captured (cap hit). Largest content holder due to MSD Studio Archive (169+ pages). 4-section IA: course planning, enrolment, subject information, student experience.

nodes.label: Faculty of Arts
nodes.description: CS at arts.unimelb.edu.au/students. 91 pages (51 unique, 13 link-farm, 24 mixed, 3 broken). Best example of hub-and-spoke balance — deep-links to hub course-admin/* pages rather than restating. Genuine unique content: experiential learning, career mentoring, overseas travel grants, language placement tests.

nodes.label: Faculty of Education
nodes.description: CS at education.unimelb.edu.au/study/current-students (unique URL pattern). ~30-45 pages. Best example of hub-and-spoke balance: holds unique content (LANTITE, ICAS, iTEA, academic skills module) while redirecting transactions to hub. MT FAQ (5,447w) is the single largest page, absorbing enrolment, assessment, extensions, and special consideration.

nodes.label: Business & Economics + Melbourne Business School
nodes.description: Split CS architecture: BCom on fbe.unimelb.edu.au/students, MBS graduate on mbs.unimelb.edu.au/students (separate subdomain). /current-students returns 404. MBS Career Elevation runs parallel to central Careers hub with no cross-linking. ~25 pages estimated.

nodes.label: Engineering & IT (FEIT)
nodes.description: CS at eng.unimelb.edu.au/students. 204 pages (174 unique, 0 link-farm, 13 mixed, 17 broken). Most self-contained faculty with complete parallel ecosystem: internship infrastructure (~14K words), orientation, scholarships, career mentoring, graduate research. Only 265 hub links across 204 pages.

nodes.label: Fine Arts & Music (VCA + MCM)
nodes.description: CS at finearts-music.unimelb.edu.au/current-students. ~30-50 pages. Most self-contained and least hub-dependent faculty. Conservatorium/art-school model (ensembles, practice rooms, performances, studio practice, exhibitions) does not map to hub taxonomy. Thinnest root page (~100 words). Strongest case for faculty autonomy.

nodes.label: Melbourne Law School
nodes.description: CS at law.unimelb.edu.au/students. 195 pages (162 unique, 23 link-farm, 10 mixed). Self-contained ecosystem with parallel systems for course planning, careers, academic skills, forms, research candidature. 184 hub links across 195 pages. Unique: JD/MLM course plans, MLS Clinics (12 clinics), degree partnerships (8 international), career pathways (7).

nodes.label: Medicine, Dentistry & Health Sciences
nodes.description: CS at mdhs.unimelb.edu.au/study/current-students. ~50 pages across faculty + 3+ school subdomains (Biomedical Sciences, Dental, MSPGH). Most fragmented architecture — multiple schools run full CS sections on separate subdomains. Faculty hub is thin (4 cards). Unique: Fitness to Practice, clinical placements, immunisation compliance.

nodes.label: Faculty of Science
nodes.description: CS at science.unimelb.edu.au/students. 94 pages (91 unique, 0 link-farm, 2 mixed, 1 broken). Only faculty with 0 link-farm pages. Wide-and-shallow IA (max depth 3) with substantive landing pages. Unique: first-year subject sets (9 disciplines), course guides (BSc/BAg/DGS), quota subjects, research project subjects, Dean's Honours List, SSRC.

nodes.label: School of Biomedical Sciences (MDHS)
nodes.description: Separate CS section at biomedicalsciences.unimelb.edu.au/study/current-student-information. 42 pages (21 unique, 3 mixed, 0 link-farm, 18 redirect/broken). Two program subtrees: Bachelor of Biomedicine and Master of Biomedical Science. 16 major pages redirect to prospective catalogue. BSc program card is circular self-link. University Health Hub redirects to generic MDHS Contact.

nodes.label: Melbourne Dental School (MDHS)
nodes.description: Separate CS section at dental.unimelb.edu.au/study/student-resources. Content dynamic/JS-rendered — not fully captured. Runs its own forms repository and research/grant pages. Part of MDHS fragmentation pattern.

nodes.label: Student Life (Topic)
nodes.description: 214 tagged pages — mid-to-large topic. Almost entirely faculty-owned: clubs, mentoring, ambassador programs, awards, discipline enrichment. Inflated by MSD studio archive and Science SSRC bios. 9 content types identified from clubs/societies to studio/exhibition archives. Legitimate content is strongly faculty-owned.

nodes.label: Subjects & Timetabling (Topic)
nodes.description: 319 tagged pages — the single largest topic. MSD holds 64% (204 pages), mostly design studios and archives back to 2008. Strip MSD archive and the topic is small: a thin transactional spine plus legitimate discipline-specific catalogues. 8 content types identified.

nodes.label: Transactional Admin (Topic)
nodes.description: 227 pages across 5 sub-topics: enrolment (84), exams-results (78), special consideration (25), fees-finance (33), library (7). The hub-definitive cluster: 219/227 pages on faculty domains but only 6 on hub domain. The single clearest consolidation target. Law + Education hold 51% of pages but mostly through restatement volume.

nodes.label: Wellbeing & Health (Topic)
nodes.description: 87 tagged pages — mid-sized but deceptive. MDHS holds 51% (44 pages) but these are placement compliance (immunisations, screening, fitness-to-practice), not pastoral wellbeing. Genuine pastoral wellbeing content is ~24 pages duplicated across 6 faculties with identical landing patterns. 9 content types identified.

nodes.label: LANTITE
nodes.description: Literacy and Numeracy Test for Initial Teacher Education Students — a nationally required test for teaching students. Legally required and faculty-specific to Education. Cannot be centralised.

nodes.label: MLS Clinics (Melbourne Law School)
nodes.description: Twelve credit-bearing clinical legal education subjects: Tax Clinic, Public Interest Law Clinic, Climate Resilience Clinic, Indigenous Legal Advocacy Clinic, NDIS/Disability Benefits Clinic, Sustainability Business Clinic, Disability Human Rights Clinic, Stateless Legal Clinic, Street Law, Law Apps, Legal Internship, Law and the Environment Lab. Deeply discipline-specific.

nodes.label: FEIT Internship Infrastructure
nodes.description: FEIT's crown jewel content — ~14K words across 3 pages (Self-sourced internships 4,864w, International internships 4,609w, University-sourced internships 4,456w). A complete parallel internship ecosystem including practice hurdle and STEP program.

nodes.label: Page Classification Types
nodes.description: Four-category system: unique (wc≥160, substantive prose), link-farm (wc<160, mostly outbound links), mixed (unique prose + heavy hub redirection), redirect/broken (is404 or auth-gated bounce). Applied consistently across all faculty crawls.

nodes.label: Law Global Degree Partnerships
nodes.description: Eight international degree partnerships for JD students: Oxford (BCL/MLF), Cambridge (LLM/MCL), NYU (JD-LLM), NUS (LLM), Peking University, Leiden University, University of Amsterdam, Boston College, National Taiwan University, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, UBC. Plus Center for Transnational Legal Studies. Genuinely discipline-specific and cannot be centralised.

nodes.label: Science First-Year Subject Sets
nodes.description: Nine discipline-area subject set pages for Bachelor of Science: Mathematics & Statistics, Physical Sciences, Geography, Earth Sciences, Chemical Sciences, Engineering Systems, Psychological Sciences, Information Technology, Biological Sciences. Genuinely faculty-owned course planning content.

nodes.label: Education Master of Teaching FAQ
nodes.description: A single 5,447-word FAQ page that absorbs enrolment, assessment, extensions, and special consideration content across multiple topics. The page inflates Education's tally across exams-results, special consideration, and enrolment topics while being a single content asset.

nodes.label: ABP Travelling Studios Archive
nodes.description: Year-indexed travelling studio archive from 2008-2025 under /subject-information/travelling-studios/past-studios/. Inflates subjects-timetable and international topic counts. Individual year pages hold 1,100-2,400 words each.

hyperedges.relation: The widespread faculty restatement of centrally-owned transactional content (enrolment, exams, special consideration, fees) across 227 pages creates maintenance burden and version-drift risk. The recommendation is: declare the hub the single source of truth, replace faculty restatements with signpost links, keep only legitimate local residue (forms, local assessment rules, extensions), and implement one consistent 'Enrolment, results & admin' landing per faculty.
hyperedges.description: Cross-cutting recommendation bundle for the transactional admin spine — the single strongest consolidation case in the estate.

hyperedges.relation: Three archival bloat patterns (MSD studio archive 169+ pages, ABP travelling studios ~15 pages, Science SSRC bios ~30 pages) collectively inflate two major topics. De-archiving MSD, collapsing the SSRC roster, and reclassifying content would remove ~200 low-value pages from the current-student estate without removing any genuinely useful student-facing content.
hyperedges.description: The archival bloat cleanup: MSD's studio archive and Science's committee bios combine to swell two topics by roughly 60 pages of non-student-facing content. Addressing this is the highest-value single fix.

hyperedges.relation: The wellbeing-health topic conflates two fundamentally different content types: pastoral wellbeing (6 near-identical faculty landing pages restating central services) and clinical placement compliance (44 MDHS pages on immunisations, screening, fitness-to-practice). The recommendation separates them: hub owns the pastoral spine with one gateway, MDHS retains compliance but retagged out of 'wellbeing', and the dead Biomedical 'University Health Hub' stub is removed.
hyperedges.description: Wellbeing content strategy: separate the pastoral (consolidate to hub) from the compliance (retag, keep local) to make both findable and reduce the six-way duplication of central services.

hyperedges.relation: MDHS has the most fragmented CS architecture in the university: three+ school subdomains run separate CS sections with different URL patterns, a thin faculty hub with circular links, 18 redirect/broken Biomedical pages including 16 major redirects to the prospective catalogue and a misrouted 'University Health Hub'. The recommendation consolidates school CS sections under a unified pattern, fixes circular links, and standardises URLs.
hyperedges.description: MDHS fragmentation is the worst in the university — a student navigates 3+ different CS experiences across faculty and school subdomains. The recommendation package addresses this with standardisation, consolidation, and link fixes.

hyperedges.relation: Four of nine faculties use non-standard CS URL patterns (/current-students for ABP/MSD and FFAM, /study/current-students for Education and MDHS) versus the majority /students convention. MBS operates on a separate subdomain. The recommendation standardises all to /students with appropriate redirects.
hyperedges.description: URL standardisation across all faculties to the /students convention used by the majority, with appropriate redirects to preserve existing links.
